title: heliconia longiflora subsp. longiflora
intro: Heliconia longiflora subsp. longiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Heliconiaceae, commonly known as the Lobster Claw due to its brightly colored, lobster-like flower bracts. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Heliconia longiflora subsp. longiflora include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
